inference_steps.text: The black team's first scoring-shot material remains grounded in three candidate replay-related windows: 49.00s-56.00s, 57.00s-59.00s, and 72.00s-77.00s.

The replay ordering is clarified by the dense-caption evidence: within 49.00s-56.00s, the goal replay is shown from two different angles, and the second replay angle is explicitly described as a higher, more traditional overhead angle. This identifies 49.00s-56.00s, not the later windows, as the interval containing the second replay of the black team's first scoring shot.

Separately, there is still grounded visual evidence at 77.00s of a referee and a nearby word on the ice, with the word region near the referee's skates, but that frame is not validated as the second replay once replay ordering is resolved.

The evidence package still does not provide a readable OCR transcription of the ice word under the referee: the 77.00s OCR entry contains no text observations, and an additional OCR attempt at 75.00s also detected no text.

Because the second replay is localized to 49.00s-56.00s but the provided evidence does not read any word under the referee in that validated replay, no answer choice can be justified from the supplied evidence.

transcript_segments.text: Minnesota did very well last night. It's a rivalry series. The puck's down here, and we're playing more North Dakota versus Gophers. Oh, a good chance right off the jump. Jayden Perron feeding it over to Reese Gaber. Those two getting the start tonight as you see the lines going across the top of your screen.

Two changes for UND in the lineup this evening. Nate Benoit and Griffin Ness draw in. And now a chance the other way. Bryce Brodzinski feeding it across. Good stuff by Person. Follow-up chance by Nelson. Batted out by Bird. I'm not sure if Bird stopped a goal right there by batting that out of the air. But action back and forth right away. Brodzinski again. His shot goes high.

Bryce Brodzinski scoring the empty netter last night. This is for the Gophers right here. A puck goes in. A good job getting that puck over and a big save on Rhett Pitlick. Poked that puck out in front as well. And the rebound just unable to get to it was Jackson Nelson. Lost it in his skates. Griffin Ness, his season debut tonight. Up to Carson Albrecht.

captions.visual: A hockey game is in progress, with Minnesota leading North Dakota 3-0 in the first period. A player in a black jersey skates with the puck in the offensive zone. He is checked hard into the boards by a player in a white jersey, causing him to fall. The play continues as the puck is cleared away from the boards.

It's a big save again. Close was so good last time. Here come the Gophers now. Their fourth line out there.

Good save by Person. A scramble in front. Jimmy Clark had the attempt. Another stop. John Middlestat had the opportunity in the slot. That puck gets knocked off his stick. The stick loses, but here's Pearson. Look at this. Watch this puck pop right out. Middlestat just fires that. A good job just in order to beat Justin Close because if he's seeing it, he's stopping it.

Over 140 minutes now, and that one nearly goes through to stop that shutout streak. A deflection in front off the centering feed. Middlesat got the puck knocked away by Berg. Five seconds to go. McLaughlin, it got too close. He made the follow-up save on Gaber, and the period will expire. His spot has 1.24 left on the red-pit lick penalty to try and break the ice here in game number two and score their first goal of this weekend.

which you thought it was going to be hard to beat justin close i know close had pointed at something right here take that shot you got people in front or just drive in and find a man what a stuff by close denying bird from tight quarters it can be one that's not called look at blake right here this is clearly a hook
